mcqs.question: What is the worst-case time complexity of linear search?
mcqs.explanation: In the worst case, linear search must check every element in the array (when the element is at the last position or not present), giving O(n) time complexity.

mcqs.question: Linear search can be applied to:
mcqs.explanation: Linear search works on both sorted and unsorted arrays because it sequentially checks each element regardless of the ordering.

mcqs.question: What is the best-case time complexity of linear search?
mcqs.explanation: The best case occurs when the target element is found at the first position, requiring only one comparison, giving O(1) time.

mcqs.question: In an array of 100 elements, what is the average number of comparisons for a successful linear search?
mcqs.explanation: On average, a successful linear search examines half the elements. For 100 elements, the average number of comparisons is (n+1)/2, approximately 50.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is an advantage of linear search over binary search?

options.text: Does not require the data to be sorted
Uses less memory than binary search
Always finds elements in O(1) time

mcqs.explanation: The main advantage of linear search is that it does not require the data to be sorted. Binary search requires a sorted array as a prerequisite.
